title: podman kube play with --replace, multiple secrets, and machine fails with an unmarshal error
body:
### Issue Description

For this example, `secrets.yml`:

```yaml
---
kind: Secret
apiVersion: v1
metadata:
  name: user-dev
data:
  password: ZGV2
---
kind: Secret
apiVersion: v1
metadata:
  name: user-dev2
data:
  password: ZGV2
```

When I run a `podman kube play --replace` with secrets that don't exist, I get this output:

```sh
$ podman kube play --replace ./secrets.yml
Pods stopped:
Pods removed:
Secrets removed:
Error: no secret with name or id "user-dev2": no such secret
Volumes removed:
Secrets:
880e47c7a8351d1acb3007c33
6e26b45c0942bd3a8bc11f006
```

Interestingly, it only gives that error message on secrets after the first.  This actually doesn't cause any problems, and still exits with a 0 status.  Where it causes problems is with a `podman machine`:

```sh
$ export CONTAINER_HOST='unix:///home/work/.local/share/containers/podman/machine/qemu/podman.sock'

$ podman kube play --replace ./secrets.yml
Error: unmarshalling into &entities.PlayKubeReport{Pods:[]entities.PlayKubePod(nil), Volumes:[]entities.PlayKubeVolume(nil), PlayKubeTeardown:entities.PlayKubeTeardown{StopReport:[]*entities.PodStopReport{}, RmReport:[]*entities.PodRmReport{}, VolumeRmReport:[]*entities.VolumeRmReport(nil), SecretRmReport:[]*entities.SecretRmReport{(*entities.SecretRmReport)(0xc00047e160), (*entities.SecretRmReport)(0xc00047e1a0)}}, Secrets:[]entities.PlaySecret(nil), ServiceContainerID:"", ExitCode:(*int32)(nil)}, data "{\"Pods\":null,\"Volumes\":null,\"StopReport\":[],\"RmReport\":[],\"VolumeRmReport\":null,\"SecretRmReport\":[{\"ID\":\"\",\"Err\":\"no secret with name or id \\\"user-dev2\\\": no such secret\"},{\"ID\":\"\",\"Err\":\"no secret with name or id \\\"user-dev\\\": no such secret\"}],\"Secrets\":null,\"ServiceContainerID\":\"\",\"ExitCode\":null}\n": json: cannot unmarshal string into Go struct field SecretRmReport.SecretRmReport.Err of type error
```

This fails with error 125, and kills my pipeline.  As expected, when I have the secret already created, it recreates it just fine.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

1. Create a `secrets.yml` file with multiple secrets.
2. Create a `podman machine`.
3. Export `CONTAINER_HOST` to communicate with that machine.
4. Run `podman kube play --replace secrets.yml`

### Describe the results you received

An unmarshal error.

### Describe the results you expected

No error.

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

comments.body:
Thanks for reaching out!
This issue has been fixed in the development branch and will be released with the upcoming Podman 4.7 release.
